questions.q: A Lie group is best described as:
choices: a finite group together with a topology
a smooth manifold that is also a group, with multiplication and inversion smooth
any topological space with a continuous group law
a vector space equipped with a bilinear bracket
questions.explain: A Lie group is simultaneously a smooth manifold and a group, where the multiplication $\mu:G\times G\to G$ and inversion $\iota:G\to G$ are smooth maps.

questions.q: What is the real dimension of $\mathrm{SU}(2)$?
questions.explain: $\mathrm{SU}(n)$ has real dimension $n^2-1$. For $n=2$ this gives $3$, consistent with $\mathrm{SU}(2)\cong S^3$.

questions.q: What is the real dimension of $\mathrm{SO}(n)$ for $n=4$?
For a matrix Lie group $G$, the Lie algebra $\mathfrak{g}=T_eG$ carries a bracket given by:
questions.explain: For matrix groups the Lie bracket on $\mathfrak{g}$ is the matrix commutator $[X,Y]=XY-YX$, which is automatically antisymmetric and satisfies the Jacobi identity.

questions.q: Let $X=\begin{pmatrix}0&-\theta\\\theta&0\end{pmatrix}\in\mathfrak{so}(2)$ with $\theta=\pi$. Compute the $(1,1)$ entry of $\exp(X)$.

questions.explain: The identity $\det(e^X)=e^{\operatorname{tr}(X)}$ holds for any square matrix. With $\operatorname{tr} X=0$ we get $\det(\exp X)=e^0=1$, confirming $\exp$ maps $\mathfrak{sl}_n$ into $\mathrm{SL}_n$.

questions.q: The adjoint action $\mathrm{Ad}:G\to\mathrm{GL}(\mathfrak{g})$ is defined by:
questions.explain: The adjoint representation is conjugation: $\mathrm{Ad}(g)X=gXg^{-1}$ for matrix groups. Its differential at $e$ is $\operatorname{ad}_X(Y)=[X,Y]$.

questions.q: How many roots does the root system $A_2$ (that is, $\mathfrak{sl}_3(\mathbb{C})$) have?
questions.explain: The roots of $A_n$ are $\varepsilon_i-\varepsilon_j$ for $i\ne j$ in $\{1,\dots,n+1\}$, giving $(n+1)n$ roots. For $A_2$: $3\cdot 2=6$.

questions.q: In $\mathfrak{sl}_2$ with basis $h,e,f$ satisfying $[h,e]=2e$, what is the eigenvalue of $\operatorname{ad}_h$ on $e$?
questions.explain: $\operatorname{ad}_h(e)=[h,e]=2e$, so $e$ is an eigenvector of $\operatorname{ad}_h$ with eigenvalue $2$. This eigenvalue is the root $\alpha(h)=2$ for $A_1$.
